feed.title: Showcasing people, their news & creating connections | eeMe
feed.description: A space for talents, brands and the audience to connect.

entry.title: Maxim Khalil
entry.summary:
Maxim Khalil is a Syrian actor and talented artist. His entry into the industry took off after he worked as an assistant director for the series “Al Bahr Ayoub” and “Al Fawares w Al Bawasil.” In the year 2000, he began acting and has since taken part in a number of successful TV series, including “Rabe' Qortoba,” “Maraya,” “Ahl El Gharam,” “Zaman El Khof,” “Laenat Altiyn,” “Ghadan Naltaqi,” “Ruby,” and many others.

His debut in the Egyptian market was through the 2012 series “Al Shak” alongside May Ezz Eldin. Khalil also played the voice of Muhanad in the dubbed version of the Turkish series ""Nour.""

Aside from TV series, Khalil has also played a role in many films, including “The Day of the Verdict,” “The Strangers,” “Suhad,” and “Sadeeqe El Akheer.”

Khalil has certainly been keeping fans entertained up until this day. In 2020, he took part in the series “Awlad Adam,” “Al-Minasa S2,” “8 Days” and then “Al-Minasa S3” in 2021. In 2022, he played the lead role in the series “Rakset Matar.” In 2023, he played a role in the series “Ebtasem Ayoha El General.”

Over the course of his career, Khalil has taken home many awards, including the Adonia Award for his role in the series “Zaman El Aar” and a Murex d’Or Award for his role in the series “Ruby.”

entry.title: Dorra Zarrouk
entry.summary:
Dorra Zarrouk is a Tunisian actress. In 2007, she caught the attention of director Youssef Chahine who offered her a role in the film “Hay Fawda,” which marked her point of entry into the world of Egyptian cinema. That same year, she took part in two other films, namely “Al Awla Fi El Gharam” and “El Hob Keda.”

She has since taken part in many movies such as “Genenet Al Asmak,” “Laylet El Baby Doll,” “Mosawer Qateel,” “Haflet Montasaf El Leil,” “B Tawqeet Cairo,” “Sheikh Jackson,” and others. Zarrouk has also taken on roles in many successful TV series including “Al Rayyan,” “Adam,” “Lahazat Harega 3,” “Zay El Ward,” “Mazaj El Khair,” “The Maestro,” “Bala Daleel,” “El Muttahama,” and others. In 2022, she took part in the play “Hatta La Yateer El Dukan” and the film “Godran.”

entry.title: Hussain Al Jasmi has released a new track
entry.summary:
Emirati singer Hussain Al Jasmi recently released a new track on YouTube titled “Sobna.” The song, which was done in collaboration with Manea bin Saeed Al Otaiba, has a Bedouin feel to it. The song was composed by Al Jasmi, written by Manea bin Saeed Al Otaiba, distributed, mixed and mastered by Hossam Kamel, and recorded and performed at the Emirates Arts Studios in Dubai.

Not too long ago, Al Jasmi released the track “Ostaz Fl Donya,” which amassed over a million views within days of its release.

entry.title: Rabeh Sager
entry.summary:
Rabeh Sager is a Saudi singer, musician, composer and arranger. In 1983, he released his first album titled “Ya Nassim El Leil” which was followed by the album “Sabreen.” Sager was the first Gulf artist to sing in the Egyptian dialect in songs like “Takke” and “Aakhbaro Eh.”

He has also released songs in the Iraqi dialect such as “Shof Esh Sawa,” which was a major turning point in his career. He was among those who composed the Jenadriyah Operetta. He has worked on music albums for a great number of Gulf singers including Mohammad Abdo, Nawal Al Kuwaitiya, and others.

He has released several successful albums over the course of his career, including “Aktob Laha 7aref,” “Rabeh 95,” “Rabeh 96,” “Mtl El Zaman,” and others.

In recent years, Sager has released several tracks. These include “La Bas,” “Meahdenak‏,” “Ehna Saudieen,” and “Qemt El Eshreen” in 2020, “Gablet El Madeh,” “Saleel El Ezz,” and “Dima” in 2021, and “Akfah Janahak,” “Fehawi,” and “La Ebtasam Salman” in 2022.

entry.title: Hayat Al Fahad
entry.summary:
Hayat Al Fahad is a Kuwaiti actress who is one of the most prominent faces in the Gulf television and theatrical scene. She kickstarted her career in 1964 in the series “The Bou Jasoum Family.” Before that, she appeared in the play “Al Dahiya” in 1963.

She has appeared in many works since then with roles that range between comedy and tragedy. She has participated in many TV drama series including “Ruqaya W Sabika,” “Ala El-Donia El-Salam,” “Khalti Qomasha,” “Albet Bet Abuna,” “Jarah El Zaman,” “Sah El Nom Ya Arab,” “Souq El Maqasis,” “Umm Haroun,” “Margaret,” “B Tawqeet Mecca” and “Sanawat El Greesh” in 2022.

Over the course of her career, she has won many awards including “Best Actress In A First Role” for the play “Iza Tah El Jamal.

entry.title: Riham Abdel Ghafour
entry.summary:
Riham Abdel Ghafour is an Egyptian actress who has played a variety of roles throughout her career. She ventured into the acting industry in the year 2000. At the time, she took part in season two of the series “Zezenia” alongside a number of Egyptian actors such as Yehia El-Fakharani, Hala Sidqi, and director Gamal Abdel Hamid. The following year, she took on a minor role in the drama series “Hadith El Sabah” and “Al Masa.”

In 2002, she took part in her first film titled “Sehr El Oyoun” with director Fakhreddine Najida. She then took on a major role in the series “Al Raqs 3ala Salalom Motahareka” and then in the series “Al 3amma Nour” alongside Nabila Obaid and Izzat Abu Auf.

The year 2005 was great for Abdel Ghafour’s career. She took part in a number of TV series and movies such as the show “Men Gheir Ma'ad,” and the movies “Kan Yom Hobak” and “Gai Fel Saree'a” alongside Hassan Hosni and Majed Al Kadwani.

Over the course of her career, the actress has starred in numerous movies. These include, but are not limited to, “Alaya el-Tarab bet-Talata,” “Agamista,” “Al Khaliyya,” “Souq El Gom3a,” “AlKaisr,” “As For The Weather,” and others. She has also starred in a number of shows such as “Hima Ayyam Aldihk Wal Dumu'u,” “Afrah Iblis,” “Msh Alf Layla W Layla,” “Seret El Hob,” “Ela Ana,” “Ramadan Kareem,” “Qasr El Nile,” and others.

In 2022, the actress participated in a number of series, including “Wish Wa Dahr,” “The Killing,” and “Room 207.” In 2023, she took part in the series “Midlife Crisis.”

entry.title: Qusai
entry.summary:
Qusai Kheder, known by his stage name Qusai, is a Saudi-American hip-hop artist, singer/songwriter, record producer, rapper, television personality, voice actor and DJ. His passion and skill for DJing began at the age of 15 before moving to the United States to attend university. His track “Jeddah (My Hometown City)” was released as part of his first independent album titled “The Life of a Lost Soul” and did not fail to resonate with his audience back home. In 2005, he moved back to Saudi Arabia and signed with Platinum Records under the stage name Qusai. In 2008, he released the album “Don Legend the Kamelion,” topping the charts in Saudi Arabia. In 2010, he released the album “Experimental Edutainment,” which sold over 30,000 copies. Other albums include “The Inevitable Change,” ""BassLine ep"" and ""Saudi ep.”

Some of his most popular tracks include “The Wedding,” “The Job,” “Dream,” “Mother,” “Hayo El Saudi,” “Any Given Day,” “Father,” and others.

Aside from his music, Qusai has also co-hosted a television show titled “Hip Hop Na” on MTV Arabia as well as “Arabs Got Talent” on MBC4.

entry.title: Abyusif
entry.summary:
Youssef Altay, also known by his stage name Abyusif, is an Egyptian rapper who blends hip-hop and rock in his music. Some of his tracks include “Kan Fi Sora,” “Ha3,” “Deep,” “Feha Lakhfeha,” “Tag,” “Wasa3,” “Neftas,” “8 8 G,” “Malaksh Da3wa,” “Tata,” “Mamlaka,” “Oh La La,” “3ashra,” “Megatron,” “2otta,” “Matboosesh 3al Sora Kteer,” and others.

In 2022, Abyusif released his album “Ras Shitan” with 9 solo tracks, combining several genres and styles. Other albums include “7abek Bors,” “Dabadeeb,” and “Y.F.G.Y.Y.”

entry.title: Bahaa Sultan
entry.summary:
Bahaa Sultan is a well-known Egyptian singer who kickstarted his career in 1989 when he released the song “Helf” as part of a joint album with other artists. He has since released six albums, the most popular of which is “Oum Ou2af.” He has collaborated with many well-known composers and poets, leaving a distinctive mark on the artistic scene over the course of his career.

In 1999, he released his first album titled “Yali Mashi.” He has released other albums over the years including “3 D2aye2,” “Malna,” “Mindoor 3ala El Saada,” and “Bed3e Ya Rab.”

He has sung in several series including “Souq El Khodar,” “Dawaran Shubra,” “Khorm Ibrah,” “Mazaj El Khair,” “Ibn Halal,” “Al Kif,” “Foq El-Sahab” and “Son of the Poor.” Some of his other singles include “Alby Ya Raby,” “Ana Mish Maahom,” “Elly Fe Einy,” and “Wee Benques Hayaty Maak.” He also released a patriotic song titled “Enti El Ghalya.”

His latest album is titled “Cigarette.” It is comprised of ten songs including “Ana Ghaltan,” “Ta3ala Adalla3ak,” “Meen Et3allem Bebalash,” and “Delwaqti Agabnako.”

entry.title: Ahmed El Sakka
entry.summary:
Ahmed El Sakka is a well-known Egyptian actor who has multiple talents across various fields. His first breakthrough in the acting industry was through his participation in the series “Al Nawa.” Soon after that, he took part in the successful TV show “Zawaj 3ala Wara Sulivan.” He made his way onto the big screen by taking on minor roles in films such as “Hoda Wa Maali Al Wazeer” and “Al Morakabe.” Soon enough, he began taking bigger roles in several other films such as “Sa'eedi in the American University,” “Hamam fi Amsterdam,” “Short w Fanelah w Cap,” “Africano,” “Mafia,” “Tito,” “Harb Atalia,” “About Love and Passion,” “Taymour W Shafiqa,” “Al Jazira,” “Ibrahim Al Abyad,” “Harb Karmouz,” and many others.

El Sakka has taken part in several TV series as well including “Nesf Rabee AlAkhar,” “Man Allazi La Yoheb Fatma,” “Tareeq Al Ahlam,” “Ser El Lo3ba,” “Lahazat Hareg 2,” “The Black Horse,” “El Ekhtiyar,” and others.

Aside from acting on the screen, El Sakka has also taken part in several plays such as “Alabhanda,” “Afroto,” “Keda Ok” and “Sayedati Al Gamela,” which was shown at Riyadh Season.

El Sakka has also presented the program “Ighlab El Sakka” and was a jury member during the Nile Festival for Dramatic Creativity.

He previously ranked on a Forbes Middle East list of the 10 most powerful Arab male actors.

entry.title: Mona Zaki
entry.summary:
Mona Zaki is an Egyptian actress who made her debut on Egyptian television through the series “Zawaj 3ala Wraq Sulivan” and “Al 3a2ela.” She has since won the hearts of viewers with her impeccable acting skills.

She made her way to the big screen with roles in several films such as “Idhak Al Sora Tetlaa Hilwa,” “Sa'eedi in the American University,” and “Leh Khalaytni Ahebak.” She has also participated in the films “Africano,” “Mafia,” “Sahar El Layali,” “Awlad El 3am,” “Dam El Ghazal,” “Ehke Ya Schehrezade” “The Days of Sadat,” and “Taymor W Shafiqa.”

She has also starred in several series including “Layali El 7elmeya,” “Zezenia,” “Khalty Safeyya Wa AlDeir,” “Asia,” “Afrah El Qobba,” “Why Fall in Love Again?,” “Number 2,” and “Newton’s Game.”

Over the course of her career, Zaki has won many awards including the Best Actress award for her role in the films ""Awlad El 3am"" and ""Dam El Ghazal.” She has also acted in many successful plays such as “Le3eb 3eyal,” “Ya Mesafer Wahdak,” “Afroto,” and “Keda OK.”

More recently, she has played a role in two films titled “Apple of My Eyes” and “Perfect Strangers,” which were shown on Netflix.

entry.title: Samira Said
entry.summary:
Samira Said is a Moroccan singer who has certainly left her mark in the industry. At the start of the 1970s, she moved to Egypt, where she collaborated with musician Baligh Hamdi and released several songs and albums, most notably the songs “Khalas Habina” and “Ahlam Al-Amira” and the albums “Toh” and “Malesh Enwan.”

Some of her most popular songs are “Aal Gani Baad Yomen” and “Mosh Hatnazel Aanak.” Over the course of her career, Said has released many successful albums including “Youm Wara Youm,” “Aweeny Beek,” “Ayam Hayati,” “Bade Eesh,” and others.

In 2022, the singer released several tracks including “Masterayahtesh,” “Yalla Rouh,” “Korbag,” “Mahassalsh Haga,” and “Yammi,” which amassed over a million views just days after its release.

Aside from singing, Said was a jury member on The Voice Arab and The Voice Senior on MBC. In 2017, she was featured on a list of the 100 Most Famous Arab Personalities by Forbes Middle East.

Throughout the years, the singer has received many awards including four awards from the Arab Song Festival in Cairo for “Best Arab Singer,” the World Music Award, and the BBC Award for “The Best Female Artist in the Middle East,” following the release of her album “Youm Wara Youm.”

She received the ""Leader's Medal"" from the King of Morocco, and was honored for her artistic career during Murex D'or in Beirut. She also took home a Murex D'or award for “Best Arab Artist.”

entry.title: Issam Al Najjar
entry.summary:
Issam Al Najjar is a Jordanian artist who ventured into the world of music by recording songs for other singers in his home. In less than a year, he released his first track titled “Hadal Ahebik,” garnering millions of views on YouTube and TikTok.

In April 2021, he collaborated with Spotify which eventually led to the release of an English version of his song “Turning Me Up” which he released alongside Ali Ghati and Loud Luxury. He became the first Jordanian artist to achieve international popularity with an Arabic song. His song topped a list of the “50 most popular songs in the world” on Spotify. It was also one of the most searched songs on Shazam and one of the most shared on TikTok.

Some of his most popular tracks are: “Hadal Ehebik,” ""Si Tu Vuelos"" with Donna Paola, and ""One of a Kind.” He has released an album titled “Baree” which consists of ten songs.

entry.title: Abeer Nehme
entry.summary:
Abeer Nehme is a Lebanese artist who is distinguished by her powerful voice and diverse singing style. The artist is also a professional qanun player. With a bachelor's degree from the University of USEK in oriental singing, Nehme has proven her place in the industry over the years. She is the first Arab artist in the Middle East to sign a production contract with Universal Music Mena.

She has previously presented two seasons of the documentary People’s Music thnopholiaE on Al-Mayadeen channel, in which she discussed the origins of music from different countries of the world. She has also starred in the show “Aswad W Abyad,” the largest musical to take place at the Sheikh Jaber Al-Ahmad Cultural Center in Kuwait.

Nehme was chosen by the Culture and Tourism Authority in Abu Dhabi to compose and prepare the music for the Sheikh Zayed Book Award. She also prepared and sang the music for the “Al-Mutanabbi Traveling,” through which she presented a collection of sung poems by the poet Al-Mutanabbi. 

She has not caught a break in recent years. Some of her most recent tracks are “Waynak” and “Bi Saraha.”

Her talent has certainly not gone unnoticed. She has won several awards, including the Murex D'or award three times in a row for the best Lebanese song for her track “Waynak,” which garnered more than 4.5 million views on YouTube in a few months. She also received an Honor Award in Greece in 2007 and participated as a member of the jury at the Babylon Film Festival in Sardinia.

entry.title: Wael Jassar
entry.summary:
Wael Jassar is a Lebanese artist who in his early days was referred to as “a miracle child” after taking audiences by storm with his performance and charming voice. His real breakthrough in the industry came following the release of his 1996 album “Mashi.” He has since released several successful albums such as “Msheet Khalas,” “Tewadny Leih,” “Senin Odam,” and “3omry W Zekrayat.”

He has also released many songs and music videos such as “Ghariba El Nas,” “El Hekaya,” “Fe Khatwetik Seketi,” “Aatazreene,” and “Shaklo Beyhazar.”

Throughout the course of his career, Jassar has won several awards including the Murex D’or for “Best Arabic Song” for “Ghariba El Nas” in 2010. He also won an award for “Best Album” during the Alexandria festival and the Middle East music award for “Best Arab Singer” in 2011.

Some of his other tracks including “Law Tkhasmny,” “Ana Mesh Mesadaa,” and “Wala Fe Ahlam.

entry.title: Hiba Tawaji
entry.summary:
Hiba Tawaji is a Lebanese singer and theater actress. She kickstarted her career following her participation in season 4 of The Voice French, grabbing the attention of audiences for her work in pop, jazz, and opera music.

She studied acting at Stella Adler and the New York Film Academy in the United States. She has participated in four Rahbani plays, in which she sang and acted. She is proficient in Arabic, French, and English, making her quite the gem in the industry.

In 2007, she began working with distributor and composer Osama Rahbani and consequently released three albums with him. In 2008, she released the song ""Metil El Rih,” written by Mansour Al-Rahbani for the play ""The Return of the Phoenix."" In 2011, she released her album ""Don Quixote"" and then in 2012, she released yet another album titled “La Bidayi Wala Nihayi.” Two years later, she released the album ""Ya Habibi"" and the album ""Hiba Tawaji 30,” which included several songs such as ""Wahdi Lahali"" and “Tir w Aalli.”

In 2014, Al-Rahbani presented a live singing show to the public, in which Hiba sang. Over the course of her career, she has participated in several festivals, including the Dead Sea Festival in Jordan in a concert titled “Rahbani Summer Night,” the Byblos International Festival in Lebanon, and the Batroun International Festival in Lebanon, where she performed several songs from her albums.

Tawaji has also toured China, during which she starred in 69 performances of the play ""Notre Dame de Paris.” She has worked with Andrea Bocelli during the Carthage Festival and released a duet with the international artist at the International Cedar Festivals.

She has also worked with Disney as the voiceover for Princess Jasmine in the French version of the film.

entry.title: Amr Diab
entry.summary:
Amr Diab is an Egyptian star who is known for his impeccable talent which encouraged him to take part in festivals when he was just three years old.

Later in life, he joined the Higher Institute of Arabic Music in Cairo. After that, he released his first album ""Ya Tariq,” which was followed by three albums: “Ghanny Men Albak,” “Hala Hala,” and “Khalseen.” His real breakthrough in the music industry was in 1996 when he released his album “Noor Al Ain.” He rose to fame soon after and has become an icon in the music industry.

Aside from singing, Diab has acted in a number of films including “Dehk Wa Le'b Wa Gad Wa Hob” with Omar Sharif, “El Afareet,” “Al Sagenatan” and “Ice Cream in Gleam.”

Diab is the best-selling artist in the Middle East who has won several awards including the World Music Award, which he received seven times.

Diab’s song “Tamli Ma’ak” caused a storm around the world; it was translated into seven languages. Throughout the course of his career, Diab has released 34 albums, the last of which was ""Ayshni,” which included ten singles and 40 music videos.

Amr Diab entered the Guinness Book of Records, topped a Forbes list of the 100 most important personalities, and was ranked among the top 10 Arab singers in 2017.

entry.title: Najwa Karam
entry.summary:
Najwa Karam is a Lebanese artist who made a name for herself in the music industry following her participation in a competition called “Lebanon Nights,” in which she took home the gold medal. In 1989, she released her first album, ""Ya Habayeb.” She then released her second album, ""Shams El Ghanya” in 1992. Since then, fans have dubbed her “The sun of Arabic songs.”

Karam’s powerful voice has allowed her to win the hearts of many music fans in the Arab region. She sang alongside Wadih Al Safi in the song “Wa Kberna” and alongside Melhem Barakat in the song “Rah Yebka El Watan.”

Over the course of her career, she has released many successful albums and has collaborated with many writers and composers. In fact, the artist has sold more than 60 million albums worldwide.

She has won many awards including the Jerash Award, the Carthage Festival Award, the Muscat Festival Award, and the Lions Award for Best Female Singer in Lebanon. She was also honored by Rotana for releasing “the best albums.” Karam has participated in massive festivals such as Carthage, Doha, Jerash and Hala February.

Aside from singing, Karam was also a member of the jury of season one of Arab’s Got Talent on MBC4. Karam’s talent has certainly not gone unnoticed. She was ranked 5th in a Forbes list of “100 Best Personalities.” Her name also appeared on a list of “15 most inspirational women in the Middle East” as she is a humanitarian at heart who supports many charitable campaigns.

entry.title: Daniella Rahme
entry.summary:
Daniella Rahme is a Lebanese model, TV presenter, and actress. Born in Australia, she returned to Lebanon in 2010 and participated and won the crown for Miss Emigrants. After that, she took part in “Dancing with the Stars,” taking home an award as well. Soon after, the offers began rolling in and she decided to stay in Lebanon to further advance her career.

In 2015, she presented the program “The X Factor" on MBC alongside Egyptian journalist Basil Al-Zarro. She ventured into the acting industry in 2017 and has proven her talent through her participation in several drama series including "Beirut City,” "Tango,” "The Writer,” "DNA," "Adam's Sons," "Al-Awda" and the last of which was the series “Lil Mout.” In 2019, she received the “Best Lebanese Actress Phenomenon Award” during Murex d’Or for her role in the series Tango.

Aside from acting, Rahme has collaborated with brands over the course of her career. In 2017, she was chosen to be an ambassador for L'Oréal Paris in the MENA region.

entry.title: Wael Kfoury
entry.summary:
Wael Kfoury is a Lebanese artist who ventured into the world of music back in 1992 following his participation in Studio El Fan, during which he won the gold medal and the admiration of fans.

The artist achieved remarkable success after the release of his first album titled “Shafouha W Saroo Y2olou” in 1994. He then climbed to fame following the release of songs in the Egyptian dialect, including “Be3teriflik.” He also grabbed the attention of many with his duet with Nawal El Zoghbi titled “Min Habibi Ana” which caused a storm around the Arab world. He has been making waves in the music industry since.

In 2004, he released the album “Qarabe Laya,” winning several awards including “Best Album of the Year,” “The Golden Lion Award for Best Music Video” at the Cairo Festival, “The Platinum Disc,” and two Murex d’Or Awards for “Best Lebanese Singer” and “Best Music Video.” In 2006, he released the track "Hobbak Azab,” winning the admiration of many and ranking first on several radio channels. Kfoury’s 2007 album "Bihinn" also achieved remarkable success as did his 2008 album “Bahebak Ana.”

In 2009, Kfoury released a series of singles including “Law Hobna Ghalta,” “Awlak Ghalat,” and “Hekm El Alb,” all of which won the hearts of fans across the Arab world. The track “Law Hobna Ghalta” won the Murex d'Or award for “Best Lebanese Singer” and “Best Lebanese Song.”

Kfoury’s influence in the music industry is not just limited to releasing tracks. The artist was a jury member for Season 3 of Arab Idol.

Among his most recent tracks are “Set El Kul,” “El Bint El Awiye,” and a duet with Angham titled “Bardo Betewhashni.”
